<!-- AUTOGENERATED by rimsky-docs-cli-ref from the rimsky CLI's own `help` output. Do not hand-edit. -->

# rimsky CLI reference

The `rimsky` CLI is the human- and scripting-facing front-end to the REST control API. It honors `RIMSKY_CONTROL_API` for the control-api endpoint (or `--endpoint <url>`, or a configured context via `rimsky ctx use`). This page reproduces the CLI's own `--help` output verbatim — the help text is the authoritative description of every command — so the whole command tree is readable here without invoking the binary.

## Command tree

`rimsky help` (the top-level usage):

```text
rimsky — orchestration CLI for the rimsky platform.

Dev-loop:
  run <file>            Register, deploy, instantiate in one shot
  register <file>
  deploy <ref>
  undeploy <ref>
  instantiate <ref>
  rm-instance <id>      Delete a terminal instance
  ls [templates|instances|tags]
  logs <id-or-key>      Stream events (poll-based)
  watch <id-or-key>     Live feed: events + breakpoint hits until terminal
  health

Literal API:
  template register | lint | list | get | deploy | undeploy | rm
  tag create | list | get | mv | rm
  instance create | list | get | status | delete | kill | nodes | events
  node get
  admin invalidate | reset
  messages tail | show
  backfill create | list | show | cancel
  asset list | show | materialize | versions | delete | lineage
  lineage prune
  parked list

Auth:
  auth init                    Mint admin key in anonymous mode
  auth create-key --name=... --role=...
  auth list | show | revoke | rotate | status

Context:
  ctx list | use | add | rm | current

Compose:
  compose up | down | plan | status   Reconcile a rimsky-compose.yml
                                      against an already-running rimsky

Host agent:
  agent start | status | stop      Manage the local host-agent daemon

Conformance:
  conformance executor | claim-producer | publisher | validation |
              data-processing | blob-backend | probe

Common flags (all verbs):
  --endpoint <url>     Override control-api endpoint
  -o human|json        Output format (default human)
  --no-color           Disable ANSI color
  --yes                Confirm destructive operations
  -h, --help           Show this help
```

## Command groups

One section per command group, each reproducing that group's usage (`rimsky <group>`).

### `rimsky admin`

```text
usage: rimsky admin <invalidate|reset> ...
```

### `rimsky agent`

```text
usage: rimsky agent {start|status|stop}
```

### `rimsky asset`

```text
usage: rimsky asset <list|show|materialize|versions|delete|lineage> ...
```

### `rimsky backfill`

```text
usage: rimsky backfill <create|list|show|cancel> ...
```

### `rimsky conformance`

```text
usage: rimsky conformance <executor|claim-producer|publisher|validation|data-processing|blob-backend|probe> ...
```

### `rimsky ctx`

```text
usage: rimsky ctx <list|use|add|rm|current> ...
```

### `rimsky health`

```text
Usage of health:
  -endpoint string
    	control-api endpoint URL
  -key string
    	API key (Bearer token; or set RIMSKY_API_KEY)
  -no-color
    	disable ANSI color
  -o string
    	output format: human|json (default "human")
  -output string
    	output format: human|json (default "human")
  -yes
    	confirm destructive operations
```

### `rimsky instance`

```text
usage: rimsky instance <create|list|get|status|delete|kill|nodes|events> ...
```

### `rimsky lineage`

```text
usage: rimsky lineage <prune> ...
```

### `rimsky messages`

```text
usage: rimsky messages <tail|show> ...
```

### `rimsky node`

```text
usage: rimsky node <get> ...
```

### `rimsky parked`

```text
usage: rimsky parked <list> ...
```

### `rimsky run`

```text
Usage of run:
  -endpoint string
    	control-api endpoint URL
  -instance-key string
    	instance_key
  -keep
    	leave the instance and template after creation (default) (default true)
  -key string
    	API key (Bearer token; or set RIMSKY_API_KEY)
  -no-color
    	disable ANSI color
  -no-keep
    	delete instance and template after terminal state
  -o string
    	output format: human|json (default "human")
  -output string
    	output format: human|json (default "human")
  -param value
    	k=v param (repeatable); merged over --params (later wins)
  -params string
    	JSON object or @file path
  -poll-interval duration
    	poll interval when --no-keep (default 1s)
  -service value
    	late-bound service binding: <name>=<path> or bare <name> (alias). Supplying any --service auto-starts the local agent if its ~/.rimsky/agent.pid is not live
  -tag string
    	tag to attach to the registered template
  -template string
    	name of an already-registered template (mutually exclusive with <file>)
  -terminate-after-run rimsky watch
    	create the instance with terminate_after_run=true — it self-terminates once its nodes settle, so terminal-flag polling (e.g. rimsky watch) exits. Implied by --no-keep.
  -timeout duration
    	max wait for terminal state when --no-keep (0 = unbounded)
  -yes
    	confirm destructive operations
```

### `rimsky tag`

```text
usage: rimsky tag <create|list|get|mv|rm> ...
```

### `rimsky template`

```text
usage: rimsky template <register|lint|list|get|deploy|undeploy|rm> ...
```

### `rimsky version`

```text
rimsky dev
```
